comment: Tier 0 alloc/op regression floors for the backendless DynamoDb translation micro-benchmarks (issue #459), enforced per-PR by Aws2Azure.UnitTests.DynamoDb.Persistence.TranslationAllocGateTests in the fast ci.yml unit-test leg. Each scenario maps to a maxAllocBytesPerOp ceiling on the MINIMUM managed bytes allocated per op (GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread, exact/deterministic — independent of CPU/runner noise, so the absolute floor is stable run-to-run unlike timing). Extends the 'relative > absolute' philosophy of baseline-reference.json to the in-memory translation hot paths: alloc/op is gated (per #459), absolute TIME is NOT (runner-bound noise). A ceiling of 0 opts a scenario out of gating; every gate scenario must still carry an entry (a coverage drift guard fails the build otherwise). Ceilings are seeded ~1.5x the measured min B/op so a real regression (a re-materialized intermediate buffer, a per-attribute string round-trip creeping back, a lost pooled-buffer reuse) trips the gate while minor runtime-version drift does not. Bump DELIBERATELY when a change is expected to raise allocation — never by accident (see docs/perf/README.md). Scenarios mirror CosmosFusedEnvelopeBenchmarks (decode) and Spike332/CosmosWriteEncodeBenchmarks (encode) over the lean / payload_512 / wide_20s_20n document shapes, plus the ProjectionExpression apply path (Projection.Apply — top-level zero-copy, nested map/list prune, and a mixed blend) over one representative wide item. The nested projection ceilings intentionally carry the Materialize write→parse→clone round-trip cost per nested top-level attribute (a documented, pool-safe structural cost; see Projection.Materialize).
